rationale: In the literature review, the citation is tied to a specific claim about out-of-stock behavior (switching to similar products), not just listed as generic background. That claim is then mapped directly to the paper's setting to explain increased small trash bag purchases when grocery bags are unavailable or costly, and persistence after repeal. Although it is cited in a grouped set and the authors do not reuse the 2012 paper's model, it still meaningfully informs the conceptual framing and interpretation.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Che, Chen, and Chen (2012) as part of the out-of-stock literature to motivate a substitution mechanism: policy-induced unavailability of grocery bags leads consumers to choose similar alternatives (small trash bags). It applies this mechanism to interpret spillover and carryover effects in a policy-repeal context rather than extending the cited SKU-choice model.
